decision_point: In an adult with an acute Garden Type I (valgus impacted/incomplete) femoral neck fracture, is nonoperative management or percutaneous pinning preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an adult with an acute Garden Type I (valgus impacted/incomplete) femoral neck fracture, is nonoperative management or percutaneous pinning preferred?
Options:
  1. Nonoperative management
  2. Percutaneous screw pinning
label: patient_dependent
label_rationale: Percutaneous pinning prevents secondary displacement/AVN in most, but a truly nondisplaced valgus-impacted fracture in a low-demand or high-risk patient can be managed nonoperatively, so the choice is contested.
